reviews.review_msg: Overall, he's a good professor. The lectures were solid (although, note there are no slides, you're going to have to pay attention) and he makes jokes often. Assignments were reasonable (also had a great TA). Most importantly, the final was relatively easy, and he made the complete outline and structure of it abundantly clear multiple times.

This is my last semester and 4th time taking a class with Torsten. I cannot stress enough that if there is an opportunity to take a class with him, you should take it. Without a doubt my favourite prof at SFU. He is kind, helpful, and understands that students lives are busy. He is very accommodating and passionate about what he teaches.
Honestly learned more from my TA than him. His lectures were okay, but going to a 50 min tutorial helped more than a 1.5 hour lecture from him. He is funny though

Dr Kehler makes jokes during his lectures which are funny. But he just talks and talks and talks, and usually goes off topic. The final exam for this class is easy... if you know all four plays by heart. He expects us to identify the passage by naming the play it is from, who said it, when, and why. The expectation is high for a first year course.

Talks about random things 85% of the time and only 15% being about actual course material. He says very obvious things like "keeping promises makes you trustworthy". Tutorials are very helpful, discussions in those will definitely help your understanding of the plays. His lectures do not provide a better understanding for course materials.

Great professor!! I really enjoyed his lectures; they were very engaging and funny, too! The literary classics were interesting to learn about, and he is a fair marker. I highly recommend him and will definitely take a class of his again!
*for ENGL385. Great and funny prof, Seminars mandatory but lectures no attendance- lectures can tend to be a ramble off topic but overall good prof and if you read all the novels you'll be good.

Torsten is awesome through-and-through. Hilarious and clearly passionate about his teaching. As a Shakespeare fan, this class was a breeze for me, but he makes the content very accessible anyway. My TA was great too; thanks Nicholas! Two papers and a final, both easy-breezy if you actually show up, take good notes, and do the readings.

Not a fan of Hitchcock or movies in general, but the assigned readings were excellent and so interesting. Torsten was kind, welcoming, and accommodating. Generally great to listen to and talk to. Even if you aren't interested in the content, he makes it fun. I love the class by the end of the semester.
Torsten is a great instructor! He's funny, good at teaching, and clearly loves his job. His marking is always thorough and not rushed. Definitely included in my top 3 favourite profs.

Having no lecture recordings or lecture slides is outrageous, but that's his way of getting students to attend his class. I would be mad about this, but his lectures are hilarious and really engaging so I'm not complaining. His essay prompts and final exam is really straight forward if you listen and take notes in class. I love this man.

Great prof and I thankfully had a great TA too! Course material was easy and so were the essays, the final exam was a bit difficult but I still did well in the end! I feel like people who say the marking was harsh were just facing a reality check: a lot of people including myself who took the course weren't English majors.

Would never take this class. This class was meant to make us better writer but there was nothing that could improve our writing skills. During lectures, he would only talk about the stories.Essays are marked harshly. How can some expect to write good university paper when no writing lessons are provided in lecture. However,he is nice and funny prof

The profs a cool guy (pretty generous with giving extensions), and the course is pretty on par with what I expect English Uni courses to be. Just take the feedback your TA gives you to heart (they'll be the ones marking your stuff not the prof) and you can easily come out with a B or higher. Final exam wasn't too bad either as long as you listened.

Great prof! Fun lectures (rambly like some others have said) but he clearly knows and cares about the texts and finds ways to make them relatable. I didn't find the grading to be as harsh as other people have mentioned; I heard my tutorial's average was a B which sounds...fine? Ask your TA for feedback/go to office hours and that'll help a lot too!
Great prof, rambles a bit but overall easy. TA's are very hard on essay marking.

Honestly, I loved Torsten, he is an engaging storyteller and is clearly passionate about English. Sometimes lectures felt repetitive though, but it was always a good time so I didn't mind. Only complaint is that the marking is pretty harsh, and that he has no set notes, but overall the class is pretty low effort.
I've now taken 2 courses with Torsten and they are great! He can make the most boring material interesting.

Engaging lectures due to him going on slight tangents on things that are loosely related to the course readings (he really enjoys using personal experiences or funny hypotheticals to get his points across). The course was pretty straightforward for grading; your grades may depend on a TA more than him but my TA was nice so no worries there.

He was very funny and accommodating (gave us extensions). Decent lecturer, but sometimes he rambles on about the same topic he discussed in previous classes and it ends up being very boring. Essay topics can be slightly vague (strangely worded) and open to interpretation. Loved the course content though. He did great in explaining everything.
